name: OpenPanel
what_it_does: Open-source product analytics platform combining the best of Mixpanel and Plausible. Tracks events, user journeys, funnels, retention, and real-time activity. Self-hostable with Docker or deployable to cloud. SDK-based event tracking for web, mobile, and server. Positioned as the open-source alternative to Mixpanel with a simpler setup and privacy-first approach.
use_cases: Track product events (button clicks, page views, conversions) with an open-source Mixpanel alternative that you control
Analyze user funnels and retention cohorts without sending data to third-party analytics vendors
Self-host analytics infrastructure for GDPR/privacy compliance without cookie consent requirements
Monitor real-time user activity and session replay for product debugging and UX improvement
Build custom analytics dashboards using OpenPanel's API to query event data programmatically
not_for: Teams needing Mixpanel/Amplitude feature parity immediately — OpenPanel is newer and lacks some advanced features
Marketing attribution and UTM analytics at scale — PostHog or Mixpanel have more mature attribution
Businesses without infrastructure to self-host — managed cloud tier is available but new/small
best_when: You want open-source product analytics with Mixpanel-like features, self-hosting capability, and privacy-first design without the Mixpanel price tag.
avoid_when: You need mature, enterprise-grade analytics with years of battle-tested reliability — PostHog or Mixpanel have longer track records.

auth.notes: Client ID + Client Secret for tracking SDKs. Bearer token for REST API access. Self-hosted: API keys generated in dashboard. Organization and project-scoped keys.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted: free with unlimited events. Cloud: free tier with limited events/month
pricing.paid_tiers: Cloud paid tiers for managed hosting; self-hosted is always free
pricing.notes: AGPL-3.0 licensed core is free. Self-hosting is unlimited and free. OpenPanel Cloud offers managed hosting with a free tier and paid plans for higher volumes.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: depends on your infrastructure. Cloud: rate limits per plan.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard JSON errors. SDK errors are surfaced via callbacks. Documentation is improving as the project matures.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Event ingestion is append-only — duplicate events require deduplication logic. Event ID can be used to deduplicate on the server side.
known_agent_gotchas: OpenPanel is newer than PostHog/Mixpanel — some advanced features and edge cases may have bugs or missing documentation
Self-hosting requires Clickhouse for analytics storage — setup is more complex than single-container solutions
AGPL-3.0 license means SaaS products built on OpenPanel may need to open-source their code — verify licensing for commercial use
Real-time event ingestion uses a queue — analytics data may have seconds to minutes of delay before appearing in dashboards
SDK tracking requires profile identification before user-level analytics work — anonymous vs identified events must be planned carefully
REST API for querying analytics data is less mature than event ingestion SDK — complex queries may require direct database access for self-hosted

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Self-hosted gives full data control. GDPR-compliant by design (data stays on your infrastructure). Open source and auditable.
